facts.text: Pleasant Valley CDP sits at 41°44′42″N 73°49′24″W, right where Wappinger Creek cuts through the Hudson Valley.
Route 44, known locally as Main Street, threads through the community and connects it to Poughkeepsie and Millbrook.
The area that became Pleasant Valley was first settled by Dutch colonists in the 1700s, forming the historic hamlet core that defines the CDP today.
Wappinger Creek, a tributary of the Hudson River, flows through the center of Pleasant Valley, creating a small riparian habitat that supports brook trout and herons.
The town’s name reflects its gentle landscape: “Pleasant Valley” was chosen to highlight the area’s rolling hills and scenic beauty.
Pleasant Valley’s community center, the Mill Site Museum, opened in 2026 and now hosts volunteer work days and local history exhibits.
